checks.finding: design.md ## Architecture (lines 27-60) gives a mermaid flowchart with 16 named nodes covering top-level component boundaries (HWT/SEED/CPB/DP/SP/GPA/RA new deliverables vs HLT/CP/LD/LDR/LDT/LCT/WFISKILL/WFIDOCS real existing surfaces), integration with existing components, and technology choices per the Components table cross-referenced against Layer Specifications (UX/Frontend explicitly N/A with rationale; Infra/Security populated). Verified against the live repo: the Protected-File Statement's core claim matches plugins/sdd-quality-loop/scripts/sdd-hook-guard.py's protected-suffix table exactly (lines 886-927), with only a minor line-citation imprecision (design.md cites 917-923; the 6 sdd-review-loop entries actually span 919-924) that does not affect the substantive, verified-correct claim.

Built the directed graph from the ## Architecture mermaid diagram's stated edges. No node has an outgoing edge that returns (directly or transitively) to an ancestor; the graph is a DAG. Checked specifically for a false cycle from LC and LCT denoting the same underlying file from two vantage points: even collapsing them, the relationship is 'test consumes driver library' plus 'driver's existence-guard state is observed when the test runs' - a normal test-harness/library-under-test interaction, not a reciprocal cycle.

## Data Plan (lines 146-162) specifies all three required sub-fields: Data Entities (new committed tests/fixtures/loops/brownfield-seed/ plus mktemp-scoped synthetic fixtures, explicitly naming the two real files read but never written with SHA-256-unchanged verification called out); Existing Data Affected ('none'); Migration Strategy ('none') with rationale supplied by the preceding text.

## API / Contract Plan (lines 164-367) substitutes script CLI contracts for HTTP endpoints appropriately for this feature type: exact param() surfaces for both new .ps1 scripts (domain: -Attempt/-Round/-EditSummary/-Reset, no -Feature, matching the .sh original's feature-less signature; spec: -Feature/-Attempt/-Round/-EditSummary/-Reset) plus preconditions. Verified against the live repository: the domain precheck's usage line (line 9) and attempt/round/edit-summary logic (lines 37-40) match design.md's citations. HITL/WFI-audit reference-check and check-placeholders-brownfield contracts documented in comparable detail.

design.md ## Security Boundaries (lines 473-482) names all four trust boundaries (B1 fixture vs real repository, B2 harness vs real gate semantics, B3 test payloads vs hook-guard command-line analysis, B4 WFI-audit fixture vs GitHub), consistent with security-spec.md's identical B1-B4 table (lines 14-33). Each boundary states its isolation mechanism. Data classification addressed and cross-confirmed; OWASP concerns addressed per boundary and expanded in security-spec.md's OWASP Mapping and STRIDE Analysis.

SKIP: Feature Type is 'deterministic test infrastructure + two precheck script ports (Pillar A completion)', not fullstack UI work. ux-spec.md and frontend-spec.md both declare N/A with rationale; design.md contains no ## Frontend Plan or ## Backend Plan sections to cross-check, so this check has no scoped surface, matching calibration guidance to SKIP rather than manufacture a finding.

## Test Strategy (lines 369-402) covers unit scope (HITL CHECK-stub/mocked-stdin legs, WFI-audit synthetic sweep, check-placeholders positive/negative pair), integration scope (brownfield-profile leg through the real loop driver; port proof via the two wave-1 pwsh suites' boundary crossings), and AC mapping via the Cross-Layer Dependencies table plus acceptance-tests.md's full correspondence table. Concrete planned verification evidence named throughout (suite paths, run-all both lanes, LOOP_SUITE_BUDGET_SECONDS=300, per-task before/after SKIP-count recording).

All 16 nodes referenced in ## Architecture are defined in the Components table, the 'Real surfaces exercised READ-ONLY' paragraph, or established existing components cited with file:line precision in investigation.md and requirements.md. Verified the loop-driver.ps1 line citations against the live repository: Copy-LoopFixtureScripts (line 204) lists the spec twin at 206 and the domain twin at 211 exactly as design.md states; Invoke-LoopDriveSpecRound (533) and Invoke-LoopDriveDomainRound (1083) as cited.

design.md's ## ADR Change Log (lines 133-144) references ADR-0010 and states no new ADR is needed because the greenfield/brownfield fixture-profile vocabulary is already ADR-0010's contract. Verified docs/adr/0010-loop-inventory-and-fixture-vocabulary.md lines 46-47 define brownfield with the canonical seed pre-anticipated as A6/#146's deliverable. Remaining deliverables are translations of an established pattern, not a new architectural departure.
